SELECTION PROCESS

The 2025 Gallatin County Procurement Policy and § 18-4-304, MCA, shall control this request for competitive sealed proposals. Evaluation. A selection committee will evaluate all conforming proposals.

Defects and Modifications. Gallatin County expressly reserves the right, to waive any defects and to allow modifications and supplementation of bids that are submitted within the deadline.

Rejection. Gallatin County may reject any bid that is not responsive to the requirements established in the invitation for bids.

Review. The Selection Committee will review conforming proposals as follows:

Criteria / Maximum Score
Total Cost (as % back to County) / 35
Response to requirements / 25
Experience/references / 15
Additional Services / 10
Transition Plan / 15

Revisions. Vendors submitting proposals will be accorded fair and equal treatment with respect to opportunity for discussion and revision of proposals, and such revisions may be permitted, after submissions and prior to award for the purpose of obtaining best and final proposals.

Scoring & Elimination. After scoring proposals based on the criteria herein the selection committee may eliminate one or more or all vendors from further review.

Award. The contract shall be awarded to the responsible and responsive offeror whose proposal best meets the evaluation criteria.

M.1 TPS Evaluation Factors for Award
The Bureau of Prisons (Bureau) anticipates awarding one contract to a responsible offeror whose proposal conforms to the solicitation requirements and is the most advantageous to the Government. Preference will be given to proposals that demonstrate a proven approach to managing projects of comparable size and scope, including the ability to effectively oversee multiple concurrent installations, as well as the operation and management of the physical tablet and its operating environment across numerous correctional institutions. The Bureau will evaluate each offer based on the best value, trade-off process as outlined in the Federal Acquisition Regulations, part 15.101-1- Tradeoff Approach. Proposal evaluation will be an assessment of the proposal and each offeror's ability to perform the prospective contract successfully. The BOP will evaluate all competitive proposals and then assess their relative qualities solely on the factors and sub-factors specified in the solicitation. Evaluations will note the strengths, deficiencies, significant weaknesses, and risks of each proposal, in a summary format with supporting narrative.

The Government reserves the right to award a contract to an offeror other than the offeror proposing the lowest price when the Government's evaluation determines a proposal is significantly superior from a non-price standpoint and warrants payment by the Government of a premium.

The purpose of this Statement of Work (SOW) is to secure a contractor to perform comprehensive maintenance and support for the Video Teleconferencing (VTC) and Audio/Visual (A/V) system located in building 1624, conference room 203, at 5 Eglin Street, Hanscom AFB, MA 01731. The contractor shall ensure the system remains fully functional through proactive maintenance, timely hardware replacement, on-call support, and user training.
2.0 Scope of Work
• Provide maintenance for a full audio visual and URL solution for one independent conference room that is capable of both classified and unclassified video teleconferencing (VTC) connectivity.
• Classified and unclassified VTC utilizes a single CODEC, automated periods processing hardware, and has NIPR/SIPR Teams capabilities.
• Replacement parts to the VTC AV system shall comply with the established Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) policy, Video Services Policy Security Technical Implementation Guide (STIG).
• The system will maintain GVS comparability and remain on the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) Approved Products List (APL) and be Joint Interoperability Test Command (JITC) approved.

The scope of this SOO encompasses enterprise-wide storage capabilities supporting approximately 15,000 users across roughly 250 office locations. The solution must support an initial data footprint of nearly 20 petabytes, with the ability to scale indefinitely for usable capacity and support the ongoing growth of the USAOs at approximately two percent month over month, historically. The solution must support access to data from multiple geographic locations, at the nearest logical location to the user, and enable continuity of operations (COOP) for remote users and COOP processes.
